Asiatic Lion.habitat: Dry deciduous forest, scrub forest, and savanna-like grasslands.
Asiatic Lion.distribution: Primarily in and around Gir landscape, Gujarat.
Asiatic Lion.diet: Large herbivores such as deer, antelope, and livestock when conflict occurs.
Asiatic Lion.fun_fact: The Asiatic lion is the only wild lion population left in Asia.
Asiatic Lion.summary: A smaller-maned lion subspecies with a highly restricted range in western India.

Asiatic Wildcat.habitat: Arid scrublands, grasslands, and semi-desert regions.
Asiatic Wildcat.distribution: Northwestern and central parts of the Indian subcontinent.
Asiatic Wildcat.diet: Rodents, birds, reptiles, and small mammals.
Asiatic Wildcat.fun_fact: Its coat pattern helps it blend into dry grass and sandy terrain.
Asiatic Wildcat.summary: A small wild feline adapted to open, dry landscapes and elusive hunting.

Bengal Fox.distribution: Widely distributed across the Indian subcontinent.
Bengal Fox.diet: Insects, rodents, reptiles, fruits, and small birds.
Bengal Fox.fun_fact: Its bushy tail often has a distinct dark tip.
Bengal Fox.summary: A small native fox of open country, often seen in dry plains and scrub.

Chinkara.distribution: Western and central India, especially Rajasthan and Gujarat.
Chinkara.fun_fact: Chinkara can survive with very little free-standing water.
Chinkara.summary: Also called the Indian gazelle, this nimble antelope is built for arid habitats.

Chital.habitat: Moist and dry deciduous forests, grasslands, and woodland edges.
Chital.diet: Grasses, leaves, fruits, and tender shoots.
Chital.fun_fact: The chital keeps its white spots throughout life.
Chital.summary: A highly recognizable spotted deer and one of India's most common ungulates.

Four Horned Antelope.habitat: Dry deciduous forests, hilly terrain, and open woodland.
Four Horned Antelope.distribution: Patchy distribution across central and southern India.
Four Horned Antelope.fun_fact: It is the only living antelope species with four horns.
Four Horned Antelope.summary: A shy and uncommon antelope known for its unusual horn arrangement.

Golden Jackal.habitat: Scrubland, agricultural edges, wetlands, and open country.
Golden Jackal.diet: Omnivorous: small vertebrates, carrion, insects, and fruit.
Golden Jackal.fun_fact: Golden jackals are highly adaptable and often thrive near human-altered landscapes.
Golden Jackal.summary: A versatile canid that occupies a wide range of habitats across the subcontinent.

Honey Badger.habitat: Grasslands, dry forests, and semi-arid regions.
Honey Badger.distribution: Scattered parts of northwestern, central, and peninsular India.
Honey Badger.diet: Small vertebrates, insects, reptiles, eggs, roots, and honey.
Honey Badger.fun_fact: Honey badgers are famous for their bold behavior and strong digging ability.
Honey Badger.summary: A tough, low-slung mustelid with a broad diet and strong defensive instincts.

Indian Leopard.habitat: Forests, hills, scrublands, and even peri-urban edges.
Indian Leopard.diet: Deer, wild boar, primates, livestock, and other medium-sized prey.
Indian Leopard.fun_fact: Leopards are among the most adaptable big cats in India.
Indian Leopard.summary: A powerful and elusive big cat able to survive in varied Indian landscapes.

Jungle Cat.habitat: Wetlands, reed beds, scrub, and agricultural fringes.
Jungle Cat.distribution: Across many parts of India except the highest elevations.
Jungle Cat.fun_fact: Despite its name, the jungle cat is often found in open wetlands and grasslands.
Jungle Cat.summary: A medium-sized wild cat with long legs and a preference for dense ground cover.

Nilgai.habitat: Open woodland, scrub, farmland edges, and grasslands.
Nilgai.diet: Grasses, herbs, leaves, buds, and crops.
Nilgai.fun_fact: Nilgai is the largest antelope in Asia.
Nilgai.summary: A large antelope of open country, commonly seen in agricultural mosaics and scrub.

Ruddy Mongoose.distribution: Mainly peninsular India and Sri Lanka.
Ruddy Mongoose.diet: Insects, reptiles, small mammals, birds, and eggs.
Ruddy Mongoose.fun_fact: Its reddish coat and dark tail tip help distinguish it from other mongooses.
Ruddy Mongoose.summary: A terrestrial mongoose often found foraging in scrub and lightly wooded habitats.

Striped Hyena.habitat: Dry scrublands, semi-deserts, rocky terrain, and open woodland.
Striped Hyena.distribution: Northwestern, central, and parts of peninsular India.
Striped Hyena.diet: Carrion, small vertebrates, fruit, and refuse.
Striped Hyena.fun_fact: The long mane on its neck and back can stand upright when threatened.
Striped Hyena.summary: A mostly nocturnal scavenger with a distinctive sloping back and striped coat.

Wild Boar.habitat: Forests, wetlands, agricultural landscapes, and grasslands.
Wild Boar.diet: Roots, tubers, fruits, invertebrates, carrion, and crops.
Wild Boar.fun_fact: Wild boar use their snouts to root through soil in search of food.
Wild Boar.summary: A hardy and highly adaptable omnivore found in a wide range of Indian habitats.
